varieties.name: Common Sage (Salvia officinalis) 🏆
varieties.origin: Mediterranean region (Southern Europe, Northern Africa), cultivated for 2000+ years
varieties.traits: Woody perennial subshrub, 60-90 cm tall, gray-green velvety leaves, lavender-blue flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane climate: Performs well but prefers drier conditions. Requires excellent drainage. Can struggle with high summer humidity - plant in full sun with good air circulation. Tolerates Brisbane's mild winters excellently. Harvest before flowering for best flavor.
varieties.availability: Widely available - Herb Cottage, Bunnings, local Brisbane nurseries

varieties.name: Berggarten Sage (S. officinalis 'Berggarten')
varieties.traits: Compact bush 45-60 cm, larger broader leaves than common sage, rarely blooms
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent leaf production, dense bushy growth
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Excellent choice. Mildew resistance valuable in humid summers. Full sun required. Stays compact and manageable. Hardy to mild Brisbane winters. One of best performers for subtropical Queensland.
varieties.availability: Online nurseries, specialty herb suppliers in Queensland

varieties.name: Purple Sage (S. officinalis 'Purpurascens')
varieties.traits: 30-45 cm tall, purple-tinged leaves maturing to gray-green, lavender flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Less winter hardy than common sage (Zone 6+). Requires protection in cooler Brisbane areas. Best grown as annual or in containers to move if needed. Full sun essential. Ornamental value high.

varieties.name: Tricolor Sage (S. officinalis 'Tricolor')
varieties.traits: 30-45 cm, variegated green, white, pink leaves, compact growth
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, milder flavor than common sage

varieties.name: Golden Sage (S. officinalis 'Icterina')
varieties.traits: 30-45 cm, gold and green variegated foliage, ornamental
varieties.yield_notes: Good, slightly milder flavor, citrus notes in well-limed soil
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Hardy to Zone 6, suitable for Brisbane climate. Slow-growing advantage for low-maintenance gardens. Requires bright sun for best color. Tolerates subtropical conditions better than Tricolor.

varieties.name: Holt's Mammoth Sage (S. officinalis 'Holt's Mammoth')
varieties.origin: Commercial selection for large leaf production
varieties.traits: 75-90 cm tall, vigorous fast growth, extra-large leaves 10-12 cm

varieties.name: Dwarf Sage (S. officinalis 'Compacta')
varieties.traits: 20-25 cm tall, miniature compact form, small narrow leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Ideal for containers and small Brisbane gardens. Excellent air circulation due to compact size reduces humidity issues. Perfect for apartment balconies. Zone 4-10 hardy. Propagated by cuttings only.

varieties.name: Pineapple Sage (Salvia elegans)
varieties.traits: 90-150 cm tall, bright green foliage, scarlet-red tubular flowers late summer-autumn
varieties.yield_notes: High, rapid growth in warm conditions
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: EXCELLENT for subtropical Brisbane. Heat and humidity tolerant. Attracts hummingbirds and bees. Tender perennial - may die back in cold winters but resprouts. Frost-sensitive. Full sun to part shade. One of best sages for Brisbane climate.
varieties.availability: Common in Brisbane nurseries, Herb Cottage

varieties.name: Mexican Bush Sage (Salvia leucantha)
varieties.traits: 90-120 cm tall, dense shrubby growth, velvety purple and white flower spikes

varieties.name: Cleveland Sage (Salvia clevelandii)
varieties.traits: 60-150 cm, aromatic gray-green foliage, lavender-purple flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Moderate success. Prefers drier Mediterranean climate but adaptable. Requires excellent drainage. Drought-tolerant - important in Brisbane's dry spells. Prune after flowering. May need protection from extreme humidity.

varieties.name: Mealycup Sage (Salvia farinacea)
varieties.traits: 60-120 cm, blue-violet flower spikes with powdery appearance

varieties.name: White Sage (Salvia apiana)
varieties.traits: 90-150 cm tall, silvery-white foliage, white flowers with lavender spots
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: CHALLENGING. Prefers very dry conditions - struggles with Brisbane humidity. Requires perfect drainage and minimal summer water. May rot in wet winters. Best grown in large containers with controlled watering. Zone 8-10 but humidity is limiting factor. Sacred ceremonial use - harvest responsibly.

varieties.name: Clary Sage (Salvia sclarea) 🏆
varieties.origin: Mediterranean, Central Asia, cultivated since ancient times
varieties.traits: 60-120 cm, biennial or short-lived perennial, large textured leaves, showy flower spikes
varieties.yield_notes: Good, primarily grown for essential oil and ornamental value
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane: Moderate success. Requires cold period (3+ months) for flowering - may not flower reliably in Brisbane. More suitable for cooler Queensland regions (Toowoomba, Stanthorpe). Well-drained soil essential. Cultivar 'Turkestanica' more heat-tolerant.
varieties.availability: Herb nurseries, essential oil plant suppliers

varieties.name: Common Sage (Salvia officinalis) 🏆
varieties.traits: 60-90 cm woody perennial, gray-green leaves, lavender flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: VERY CHALLENGING. Does not tolerate tropical monsoon climate well. Hates wet feet and high humidity. If attempted: Grow in large containers with excellent drainage, bring under cover during heavy monsoons. May survive as short-lived annual. Not recommended for Kerala lowlands. Better suited to cooler Indian hill stations.
varieties.availability: Limited - import seeds, very rare in Kerala nurseries

varieties.name: Clary Sage (Salvia sclarea) 🏆
varieties.traits: Biennial herb with large leaves, showy flower spikes
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: NOT SUITABLE. Successfully introduced to subtropical North Indian plains as winter annual crop (Kashmir valley produces small amounts). Requires cold period for flowering. Heavy monsoon rains cause rot. Not adapted to Kerala climate. Best grown in cooler Indian regions.
varieties.availability: Not available in Kerala; Kashmir/North India sources only

varieties.name: Pineapple Sage (Salvia elegans)
varieties.traits: 90-150 cm perennial, bright green leaves, scarlet tubular flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: BEST OPTION for tropical Kerala. Highly heat and humidity tolerant. Native to tropical regions. Frost-sensitive (perfect for Kerala - no frost). Morning sun with afternoon shade ideal. Requires regular moisture - suits monsoon climate. Protect from heavy downpours. Container growing recommended for drainage control. Most likely sage to succeed in Thiruvalla area.
varieties.availability: May be available through specialty herb suppliers; import seeds possible

varieties.name: Mexican Bush Sage (Salvia leucantha)
varieties.traits: 90-120 cm evergreen shrub, velvety purple flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: GOOD POTENTIAL. Drought-tolerant once established - handles dry periods between monsoons. Tropical to subtropical origin suits Kerala. Requires excellent drainage during monsoons. Plant on slopes or raised beds. Full sun. May perform well in Thiruvalla if protected from waterlogging. Zones 8-10.
varieties.availability: Rare in Kerala; seeds may be imported

varieties.name: Scarlet Sage (Salvia splendens)
varieties.traits: 30-90 cm, brilliant red flowers year-round in tropics
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: EXCELLENT CHOICE. Native to tropical climate - ideally suited for Kerala. Thrives in heat and humidity. Self-seeds prolifically. Year-round flowering in tropical conditions. Grows well in sun or partial shade. Perfect for Kerala monsoon climate. One of the best Salvia species for tropical India.
varieties.availability: May be available in Kerala nurseries as ornamental; seeds can be sourced

varieties.name: Mealycup Sage (Salvia farinacea)
varieties.traits: 60-120 cm, blue-violet flower spikes
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: MODERATE SUCCESS POSSIBLE. Heat-tolerant but may become leggy with too much shade or water. Prefers full sun to partial shade. Requires good drainage - critical during monsoons. Grown as annual in most areas. May reseed. Benefits from afternoon shade in intense Kerala heat.
varieties.availability: Import seeds; rare in Indian nurseries

varieties.name: Grape Scented Sage (Salvia melissodora)
varieties.traits: 150-180 cm tall, open shrub, light purple flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: GOOD POTENTIAL. Mexican origin suggests heat tolerance. Large size provides good harvest. Late summer to frost flowering (continuous in Kerala). Tea use valuable. Requires space. Good drainage essential. May adapt to Kerala conditions with proper site selection.

varieties.name: Fruit Sage (Salvia dorisiana)
varieties.traits: 90-120 cm, magenta-pink winter flowers, fruit salad aroma
varieties.growing_notes: Kerala: EXCELLENT OPTION. Tropical origin from Central America - well-suited to Kerala climate. Tolerates semi-shade (benefits afternoon shade in Kerala heat). Winter flowering in native habitat may mean year-round flowers in Kerala. Highly aromatic for tea. One of best tropical Salvias for monsoon regions.
varieties.availability: Specialty herb imports; rare but suitable for Kerala
